methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Manastu Space is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Tushar Jadhav and Ashtesh Kumar. The target operates within a sector encompassing 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs, though narrow peer valuation data at identical stage and scale remains constrained.

Valuation triangulation relied on stage-progression methodology, the sole applicable approach given data limitations. Using the company's $6M total raised as a proxy for post-money valuation, a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement yields $12M. The public comp universe in Space & Aerospace trades at a median 25.8x price-to-sales, but application was not possible absent the target's revenue disclosure—a common constraint in private company analysis. Peer-median triangulation was inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparable datapoints within the narrow space sector cohort.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $10M to $14M, centered at $12M. This range reflects stage-typical progression mechanics rather than market-derived multiples. Key uncertainties include absence of financial performance data (revenue, burn rate, unit economics), limited visibility into private peer transaction terms, and the target's specific technical and commercial positioning relative to an undifferentiated peer set. Additional revenue or customer traction metrics would strengthen triangulation considerably.
